name: MaxionBench evaluation card
evaluation_object: Agentic retrieval infrastructure under a conformance-gated CPU-only local runtime profile.
primary_claim: Agentic retrieval systems require decision-audit evaluation across conformance, quality, post-insert top-10 retrievability, max-row p99 latency, normalized context-cost proxy, budget stability, and objective sensitivity.

supported_claims.claim: Strict 200 ms max-row p99 deployment selects FAISS CPU with bge-small for S1, S2, and S3 in the archived run bundle.
supported_claims.scope: recorded CPU-only runtime profile, archived MaxionBench bundle
reported MaxionBench objectives and budget rows

supported_claims.claim: The S3 pgvector quality-first edge is indistinguishable from FAISS under matched-query audit.
S2 paired checks find no Qdrant quality or post-insert retrievability advantage.
supported_claims.scope: standard Qdrant paired check, same-orchestration mini-bundle, and larger same-machine two-repeat paired check

future_extensions: second-machine, distributed, GPU, and managed-service matrices
high-probe/high-ef service-engine sweeps beyond the targeted checks in paper/experiments

datasets.name: D4 MaxionBench S1/S2 processed text bundles

compute_scope.accelerator_use: reported matrix uses CPU rows throughout
compute_scope.public_repository_note: Host identifiers, OS patch level, user names, and machine-local paths are intentionally omitted from public files.

interpretation_notes: The included matrix uses one recorded local hardware profile.
Max-row p99 is computed from the archived configured B2 client rows and repeats and reported with the recorded hardware/runtime profile.
The S2 same-machine reruns use capped post-insert retrievability events for fixed timed phases.
The artifact evaluates retrieval infrastructure; answer-generation quality can be layered on top.
Dataset hosting URLs must be anonymized and finalized before submission.
